title: Add repository configuration diagnostics and operational error handling
objective: Provide an authenticated diagnostics experience that explains GitHub authentication, repository access, configuration, caching and artifact-loading failures.
userContext: The application currently converted a missing production filesystem into a misleading zero-artifact state. As GitHub authentication, private repository access and caching are introduced, the user needs clear diagnostics instead of silent failures.
currentBehaviour: Operational failures are inconsistently surfaced. Missing artifact storage can appear as an empty library, and there is no page showing repository configuration, permission state, latest revision or cache status.
requiredBehaviour: Authorised users must have access to a diagnostics page that reports the effective GitHub identity, repository configuration, app installation, permissions, latest repository revision, cache state and artifact validation results. User-facing errors must distinguish empty content from authentication, authorisation, configuration and service failures.
userExperience: When the library cannot load, the user sees a specific, actionable message. From a protected diagnostics page, the user can understand whether they are signed in, authorised, correctly configured, reading fresh or stale data and encountering invalid artifact files.
functionalRequirements: Show the configured repository owner, name, branch and artifact root path.
Show whether the GitHub App installation is detected.
Show effective read and write permission state.
Show the latest known repository commit or revision.
Show whether cached content is fresh or stale.
Show the number of valid and invalid artifacts.
List invalid artifact files with safe diagnostics.
Distinguish unauthenticated, unauthorised, repository-not-found, app-not-installed, insufficient-permission, rate-limit and GitHub-unavailable states.
Replace silent zero-artifact fallback for operational failures.
Ensure diagnostics do not display access tokens, private keys or secret values.
technicalConsiderations: Reuse authentication, authorisation, repository and cache services rather than duplicating checks.
Use structured internal error types or codes.
Expose only diagnostics appropriate to an authorised user.
Ensure errors are observable in Cloudflare logs without leaking secrets.
outOfScope: Changing repository permissions from the app.
Detailed GitHub billing or quota analytics.
acceptanceCriteria: An authorised user can open the diagnostics page.
The page shows GitHub identity and configured repository details.
The page reports app installation and effective permissions.
The page shows repository revision and cache freshness.
Malformed artifacts are reported without exposing private content unnecessarily.
Operational failures no longer appear as a misleading empty library.
Authentication, authorisation, configuration and GitHub service failures have distinct messages.
Secrets are never shown in diagnostics or logs.
Relevant tests, type checking and Cloudflare deployment validation pass.
The current application specification is updated for diagnostics and error behaviour.
The implementation pull request includes OPS-001 and closes the generated issue.
codexGuidance: Implement after the core authentication and GitHub repository services exist. Prefer a shared typed error model that improves both the diagnostics page and normal library error states.
